strings.c(3) Library Functions Manual strings.c(3)

NAME

strings.c - Session string generation and validation implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct word_cache_entry_t
Hashtable entry for cached word lookups.

Functions


void acds_strings_destroy (void)
Cleanup function for session string cache Called by asciichat_shared_destroy() during library cleanup. Safe to call multiple times (idempotent). asciichat_error_t acds_string_init (void)
asciichat_error_t acds_string_generate (char *output, size_t output_size)
bool is_session_string (const char *str)

Detailed Description

Session string generation and validation implementation.

This module generates memorable session strings in the format: adjective-noun-noun (e.g., "swift-river-mountain")

It maintains cached hashtables of adjectives and nouns for fast O(1) validation, ensuring session strings only contain real words.

Definition in file discovery/strings.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t acds_string_generate (char * output, size_t output_size)

Definition at line 208 of file discovery/strings.c.

208                                                                          {
209   if (!output || output_size < SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE) {
210     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "output buffer must be at least %zu bytes",
211                      (size_t)SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE);
212   }
213 
214   // libsodium is guaranteed to be initialized by acds_string_init() before this is called
215   // No need to call sodium_init() again (redundant initialization removed)
216 
217   // Pick random adjective
218   uint32_t adj_idx = simple_randombytes_uniform((uint32_t)adjectives_count);
219   const char *adj = adjectives[adj_idx];
220 
221   // Pick two random nouns
222   uint32_t noun1_idx = simple_randombytes_uniform((uint32_t)nouns_count);
223   uint32_t noun2_idx = simple_randombytes_uniform((uint32_t)nouns_count);
224   const char *noun1 = nouns[noun1_idx];
225   const char *noun2 = nouns[noun2_idx];
226 
227   // Format: adjective-noun-noun
228   int written = safe_snprintf(output, output_size, "%s-%s-%s", adj, noun1, noun2);
229   if (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= output_size) {
230     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW, "Session string too long for buffer");
231   }
232 
233   log_dev("Generated session string: %s", output);
234   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
235 }




References adjectives, adjectives_count,
    nouns, nouns_count, safe_snprintf(), and
    SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE.


Referenced by database_session_create(),
    options_preset_unified(), and server_main().

void
  acds_strings_destroy (void )


Cleanup function for session string cache Called by
    asciichat_shared_destroy() during library cleanup. Safe to call
    multiple times (idempotent).


Definition at line 60 of file
  discovery/strings.c.


60                                 {
61   // Shutdown if initialized
62   if (!lifecycle_shutdown(&g_cache_lc)) {
63     return; // Not initialized
64   }
65 
66   // Cleanup adjectives cache
67   word_cache_entry_t *adj_entry, *adj_tmp;
68   HASH_ITER(hh, g_adjectives_cache, adj_entry, adj_tmp) {
69     HASH_DEL(g_adjectives_cache, adj_entry);
70     SAFE_FREE(adj_entry->word);
71     SAFE_FREE(adj_entry);
72   }
73   g_adjectives_cache = NULL;
74 
75   // Cleanup nouns cache
76   word_cache_entry_t *noun_entry, *noun_tmp;
77   HASH_ITER(hh, g_nouns_cache, noun_entry, noun_tmp) {
78     HASH_DEL(g_nouns_cache, noun_entry);
79     SAFE_FREE(noun_entry->word);
80     SAFE_FREE(noun_entry);
81   }
82   g_nouns_cache = NULL;
83 
84   log_dev("Session string word cache cleaned up");
85 }






References lifecycle_shutdown(), and
    word_cache_entry_t::word.


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().






bool is_session_string (const char * str)


Definition at line 238 of file
  discovery/strings.c.


238                                         {
239   if (!str || str[0] == '\0') {
240     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string is NULL or empty");
241     return false;
242   }
243 
244   size_t len = strlen(str);
245 
246   // Check length bounds (min 4, max defined by SESSION_STRING_MAX_LEN)
247   if (len < 4 || len > SESSION_STRING_MAX_LEN) {
248     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string length %zu outside valid range 4-%d", len, SESSION_STRING_MAX_LEN);
249     return false;
250   }
251 
252   // Session strings must be ASCII-only (homograph attack prevention)
253   if (!utf8_is_ascii_only(str)) {
254     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string contains non-ASCII characters");
255     return false;
256   }
257 
258   // Get compiled regex (lazy initialization)
259   pcre2_code *regex = session_format_regex_get();
260   if (!regex) {
261     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Session string PCRE2 regex failed to initialize");
262     return false;
263   }
264 
265   // Validate format using PCRE2 regex
266   pcre2_match_data *match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(regex, NULL);
267   if (!match_data) {
268     log_error("Failed to allocate match data for session string regex");
269     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate match data");
270     return false;
271   }
272 
273   int match_result = pcre2_jit_match(regex, (PCRE2_SPTR8)str, len, 0, 0, match_data, NULL);
274 
275   pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
276 
277   if (match_result < 0) {
278     // Format validation failed
279     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string format does not match pattern");
280     return false;
281   }
282 
283   // Extract the three words by finding hyphens
284   // Format is guaranteed to be: word-word-word where each word is 2-12 lowercase letters
285   const char *hyphen1 = strchr(str, '-');
286   if (!hyphen1) {
287     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string missing first hyphen");
288     return false;
289   }
290 
291   const char *hyphen2 = strchr(hyphen1 + 1, '-');
292   if (!hyphen2) {
293     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string missing second hyphen");
294     return false;
295   }
296 
297   // Extract three words
298   size_t adj_len = hyphen1 - str;
299   size_t noun1_len = hyphen2 - hyphen1 - 1;
300   size_t noun2_len = len - (hyphen2 - str) - 1;
301 
302   if (adj_len >= 32 || noun1_len >= 32 || noun2_len >= 32) {
303     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string word length out of bounds");
304     return false;
305   }
306 
307   char adj[32], noun1[32], noun2[32];
308   memcpy(adj, str, adj_len);
309   adj[adj_len] = '\0';
310   memcpy(noun1, hyphen1 + 1, noun1_len);
311   noun1[noun1_len] = '\0';
312   memcpy(noun2, hyphen2 + 1, noun2_len);
313   noun2[noun2_len] = '\0';
314 
315   // Lazy initialization: build validation caches on first use
316   // call_once ensures thread-safe exactly-once execution
317   build_validation_caches();
318   // If initialization fails silently (logs warning), hashtables will be empty
319   // and validation will fail, which is safe behavior
320 
321   // Validate first word is an adjective
322   word_cache_entry_t *adj_entry = NULL;
323   HASH_FIND_STR(g_adjectives_cache, adj, adj_entry);
324   if (!adj_entry) {
325     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string first word '%s' is not a valid adjective", adj);
326     return false;
327   }
328 
329   // Validate second and third words are nouns
330   word_cache_entry_t *noun_entry1 = NULL;
331   HASH_FIND_STR(g_nouns_cache, noun1, noun_entry1);
332   if (!noun_entry1) {
333     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string second word '%s' is not a valid noun", noun1);
334     return false;
335   }
336 
337   word_cache_entry_t *noun_entry2 = NULL;
338   HASH_FIND_STR(g_nouns_cache, noun2, noun_entry2);
339   if (!noun_entry2) {
340     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string third word '%s' is not a valid noun", noun2);
341     return false;
342   }
343 
344   log_dev("Valid session string: %s", str);
345   return true;
346 }






References utf8_is_ascii_only().


Referenced by database_session_create(),
    discover_session_parallel(), and parse_client_address().
